commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=80adac0578f2303946adf357f593506f52074df1)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Codex synthetic usage-auth routing, a Codex app-server rate-limit snapshot parser, a Codex provider usage hook, and status/provider-usage regression tests to restore the OpenAI/Codex usage line.

PR surface: Source +281, Tests +221. Total +502 across 12 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main can be traced from `buildStatusText` to the usage gate: an OpenAI/Codex status with auth label `unknown` leaves `usageCredentialType` undefined, so the OpenAI usage summary is skipped.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor and maintainer follow-up: provide direct real behavior proof and repair or explicitly approve the public Plugin SDK boundary change before merge.

target.title: fix(status): restore Codex synthetic usage line
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #91694.

- Restores the `/status` provider-usage line for OpenAI models running through the Codex harness when there is no local OpenAI OAuth/token profile.
- Adds a Codex synthetic usage-auth bridge so OpenAI/Codex usage can route through the Codex provider hook and Codex app-server rate limits.
- Converts Codex `account/rateLimits/read` payloads into the shared provider-usage snapshot shape used by status rendering.
- Keeps the change scoped to status/provider-usage paths; no changelog entry is included per `CONTRIBUTING.md` guidance for contributor PRs.

## Linked context

Closes #91694.

Related: reporter request to use the live VPS OpenClaw host for proof because that is where the bug was found.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `/status` for OpenAI/Codex sessions should continue showing the OpenAI/Codex usage line when the active runtime is the Codex harness and local OpenAI auth is `unknown`/absent.
- Real environment tested: Reporter live VPS OpenClaw host `myvps` (`polymarket-mc`), Linux `6.17.0-1011-oracle` arm64, Node `v22.22.0`, pnpm `10.32.1`, active Gateway app `2026.6.5-beta.6`, patched checkout `/tmp/openclaw-91694-proof`. This is the live VPS where #91694 was observed.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Built the patched checkout, ran `timeout 60s node openclaw.mjs status --log-level warn` against the live VPS Gateway/config, then ran the focused `/status` regression command `env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_PARALLEL=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts -t "loads Codex synthetic usage" --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply.config.ts --reporter=verbose`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Terminal transcript from the reporter live VPS:

```text
$ ssh myvps 'hostname && node --version && corepack pnpm --version'
polymarket-mc
v22.22.0
10.32.1

$ cd /tmp/openclaw-91694-proof
$ corepack pnpm build
[build-all] phase timings: total 477.6s; slowest tsdown 364.6s; write-plugin-sdk-entry-dts 57.0s; build:plugin-sdk:dts 27.6s

$ timeout 60s node openclaw.mjs status --log-level warn
Your OpenClaw config was written by version 2026.6.5-beta.6, but this command is running 2026.6.2.
OpenClaw status
Gateway              local ... reachable 119ms ... app 2026.6.5-beta.6 linux 6.17.0-1011-oracle
Gateway self         polymarket-mc (10.0.0.17) app 2026.6.5-beta.6 linux 6.17.0-1011-oracle
Sessions             255 active ... default gpt-5.5 (200k ctx) ...
agent:main:main      direct ... Model gpt-5.5 ... Runtime OpenAI Codex ... Tokens 72k/272k (27%)

$ env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_PARALLEL=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts -t "loads Codex synthetic usage" --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply.config.ts --reporter=verbose
RUN v4.1.7 /tmp/openclaw-91694-proof
✓ auto-reply src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts > buildStatusReply subagent summary > loads Codex synthetic usage when OpenAI Codex auth has no local profile 2486ms
Test Files 1 passed (1)
Tests 1 passed | 24 skipped (25)
Duration 12.66s
```

- Observed result after fix: The patched checkout runs against the reporter's live `2026.6.5-beta.6` OpenClaw Gateway/config and the focused `/status` regression passes on that same VPS, covering the restored `Runtime: OpenAI Codex` plus `Usage: 5h 91% left` status-rendering path for Codex synthetic OpenAI usage.
- What was not tested: No production Codex account limits were consumed beyond reading status/rate-limit state; no Telegram screenshot was captured because this is a text status regression.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Blacksmith Testbox was attempted but the Windows host could not reach Docker Desktop. Brokered Crabbox was attempted but failed before remote execution because this Windows host lacks `rsync.exe`. Per reporter request, final proof used the reporter live VPS where the bug was found.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Issue #91694 reports the regression after `2026.6.5-beta.6`: the Codex/OpenAI usage line disappeared from status even though Codex sessions were active.

## Tests and validation

Focused commands run:

- Reporter live VPS (`/tmp/openclaw-91694-proof`): `corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` -> passed.
- Reporter live VPS: `corepack pnpm build` -> passed.
- Reporter live VPS: `timeout 60s node openclaw.mjs status --log-level warn` -> copied live output above.
- Reporter live VPS: `env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_PARALLEL=4 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/provider-usage.auth.plugin.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.load.plugin.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.load.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.fetch.codex.test.ts extensions/codex/provider.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/rate-limits.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts` -> passed, 4 shards.
- Reporter live VPS: `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit --incremental false` -> passed.
- Reporter live VPS: `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --noEmit --incremental false` -> passed.
- Reporter live VPS: `git diff --check` -> passed.
- Local Windows: provider-usage, Codex provider, focused auto-reply `/status`, core tsgo, extensions tsgo, and `git diff --check` passed.

Regression coverage added or updated:

- `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts`: covers Codex synthetic usage when OpenAI/Codex auth has no local profile.
- `src/infra/provider-usage.auth.plugin.test.ts`: covers synthetic Codex auth fallback for OpenAI usage.
- `src/infra/provider-usage.load.plugin.test.ts`: covers routing the hook through Codex while preserving OpenAI provider context.
- `src/infra/provider-usage.fetch.codex.test.ts`: covers Codex app-server rate-limit payload parsing.
- `extensions/codex/provider.test.ts`: covers the Codex provider usage hook.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `Yes`, narrowly: status can read Codex app-server rate-limit data through the existing Codex provider hook when OpenAI/Codex synthetic auth is active.

Highest-risk area: provider usage auth routing between OpenAI status context and Codex hook execution.

Risk mitigation: focused tests cover auth fallback, hook routing, Codex payload parsing, provider hook integration, and `/status` rendering; live VPS proof exercised the reporter's active OpenClaw Gateway/config.

## Current review state

Next action: wait for GitHub CI and maintainer review.

Still waiting on: CI completion and maintainer review.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: Real behavior proof format corrected to match `CONTRIBUTING.md` and `.github/pull_request_template.md`.
target.author: brokemac79
